symptoms.symptom_name: Rigid, unwanted erection lasting >6 hours, unassociated with sexual desire
symptoms.onset: after intracavernosal injection earlier in the evening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A persistent rigid erection beyond 4 hours, unrelated to arousal, is the definition of priapism; duration >4-6 hours plus pain defines the ischaemic emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe, progressively worsening deep penile pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ischaemic (low-flow) priapism is painful because of tissue hypoxia and acidosis; pain severity tracks the urgency of decompression.
High-flow priapism is characteristically painless — significant pain points away from it and toward the ischaemic emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset shortly after intracavernosal alprostadil (prostaglandin E1) injection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intracavernosal vasoactive agents are a well-recognised precipitant of iatrogenic ischaemic priapism.

symptoms.symptom_name: Early blue/dark-red discolouration along the penile shaft

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dusky discolouration signals deoxygenation of the trapped blood and advancing ischaemia — a marker that the emergency window is closing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rigid corpora cavernosa with a soft, flaccid glans and corpus spongiosum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fully rigid corpora with a flaccid glans/spongiosum is the classic low-flow anatomical pattern; requires in-person palpation to confirm and is not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cavernosal arterial blood gas showing hypoxia, hypercapnia and acidosis

investigation_details.expected_result: Low pO2, high pCO2 and low pH — confirming ischaemic (low-flow) priapism
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to distinguish ischaemic from non-ischaemic priapism and to confirm the emergency; performed in hospital, never available via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A hypoxic, acidotic cavernosal gas is the gold-standard confirmation of the low-flow emergency; a hospital investigation, never obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Difficulty initiating micturition from urethral compression

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The rigid corpora mechanically compress the urethra; voiding difficulty flags a possible progression to retention that must be safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent perineal/straddle trauma to the genitals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-flow priapism follows perineal or straddle trauma that ruptures a cavernosal artery; its absence, with pain and full rigidity, points to the ischaemic emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR ~115) and hypertension (BP ~165/100) from severe pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured tachycardia and hypertension reflect the pain crisis; a sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers distress from the patient's report, not a measured vital.
